content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 30, 1995

Douglas Hansen, Chairman
Coalition of California Home Inspectors
c/o Toltec Inspection Services
2155 Bowdoin
Palo Alto, CA 94306

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-088

Dear Mr. Hansen:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Coalition of
California Home Inspectors ("CCHI") concerning the lobbying
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Does CCHI qualify as a lobbying coalition for purposes of the
lobbying disclosure provisions of the Act?

CONCLUSION

Once CCHI has received payments from 10 or more members for
the purpose of employing a lobbyist, it will qualify as a lobbying
coalition under Commission Regulation 18616.4. The term "member"
includes individuals who contribute toward the cost of employing a
lobbyist as well as CCHI's corporate and association members.

FACTS

You provided the following facts in your letter and during
our telephone conversation of March 23, 1995.

CCHI consists of three non-profit mutual benefit associations
of home inspectors and three corporations which are either in the

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Douglas Hansen
Our File No. A-95-088
Page 2


inspection business or provide services to the inspection
industry, as follows:

                    American Society of Home Inspectors
                    California Real Estate Inspection Association
                    American Institute of Inspectors
                    Inspectech Corporation
                    Amerispec Corporation
                    Inspection Training Associates

     The six entities joined together in November 1994 for the
purpose of monitoring the regulatory activities of state agencies
and notifying individuals in the inspection industry about those
activities.  CCHI does not collect member dues and does not
provide any other services to its members.

     CCHI has decided to employ a lobbyist.  The funds needed to
employ the lobbyist will come from the corporate members and from
individual inspectors who are members of the three non-profit
associations.  You anticipate that over 100 individual will
contribute.

                              ANALYSIS

     Regulation 18616.4 establishes the filing requirements for
lobbying coalitions and was adopted by the Commission to
consolidate reporting by groups of individuals and entities which
pool funds solely for the purpose of employing a lobbyist.  Prior
to adoption of the regulation, each member of a coalition was
required to register and file reports as a separate lobbyist
employer.  Regulation 18616.4 defines the term "lobbying
coalition" for purposes of the Act as:

          ...a group of ten or more persons formed
     primarily to influence legislative or
     administrative action, whose members make payments
     to the coalition for the purpose of sharing the
     expenses of employing a lobbyist or contracting for
     the services of a lobbying firm.  A bona fide
     federation, confederation or trade, labor or
     membership organization is not a lobbying coalition
     if it is ongoing in nature and its membership
     services are not limited to influencing legislative
     or administrative action....

     The term "person" is defined in Section 82047 of the Act to
include entities such as corporations and associations, as well as
individuals.  Thus, each entity and each individual which makes
payments toward the cost of employing a lobbyist would be
considered a "member" of CCHI.  Once CCHI has received payments
from 10 or more members, it will qualify as a lobbying coalition.

Douglas Hansen
Our File No. A-95-088
Page 3

As a lobbying coalition, CCHI must be registered and file
quarterly disclosure reports. To register, CCHI must complete
Form 602 (Lobbying Firm Activity Authorization) and provide it to
the lobbying firm with which it has contracted.2/ The firm will
register CCHI as one of its lobbying clients. Each quarter, CCHI
must file Form 635 (Report of Lobbyist Employer/Lobbying
Coalition) disclosing payments made to the lobbying firm and any
other payments it makes in connection with its lobbying
activities. Form 635-C (Payments Received by Lobbying Coalitions)
must be attached to each quarterly report disclosing the name and
address of each coalition member and the amount paid by the member
during the quarter.

Members of CCHI which qualify as lobbyist employers for other
purposes must disclose payments made to the coalition on their
individual lobbyist employer reports. Members which are not
currently lobbyist employers may incur filing obligations if they
make payments totaling $5,000 in a calendar quarter to influence
legislative or administrative action. Payments made to CCHI must
be counted toward the $5,000 threshold.

Copies of the relevant forms are enclosed. If you have
questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

Carla Wardlow

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosures

_________________________

3/ For purposes of these instructions, we assume that CCHI has
contracted for the services of an individual or entity that
qualifies as a "lobbying firm" under the Act. If this is not the
case, please contact the Technical Assistance Division at (916)
322-5660 for additional information.

CCHI qualify as a lobbying coalition for purposes of the
lobbying disclosure provisions of the Act?

CONCLUSION: Once CCHI has received payments from 10 or more members for
the purpose of employing a lobbyist, it will qualify as a lobbying
coalition under Commission Regulation 18616.4.
The term “member”
includes individuals who contribute toward the cost of employing a
lobbyist as well as CCHI’s corporate and association members.
